text.title: fco-waste-elimination Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for fco-waste-elimination. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: All non-production environments (dev, staging, QA, sandbox) MUST implement scheduled shutdown during off-hours. Minimum schedule: stop at 20:00, start at 06:00 on weekdays; stop Friday 20:00, start Monday 06:00 for weekends. Target 70% cost reduction on non-prod compute.
rule.rationale: Non-production environments are used during business hours only. Running them 24/7 wastes 65-70% of their cost. The AWS Instance Scheduler, Lambda cron, or Terraform scheduled actions implement this in under 2 hours with 65-70% immediate savings.

rule.statement: Implement automated idle resource detection to run weekly. Alerts MUST include: resource ID, age, estimated monthly cost, and last-active date. Cleanup MUST require human approval for production resources; non-production cleanup can be automated with a 7-day warning period.
rule.rationale: Manual audits decay — after the initial cleanup sprint, the process is forgotten and waste re-accumulates within 3-6 months. Automated weekly scanning with human-readable alerts keeps the pipeline clean continuously.
